sections.paragraph:
i.q. שׁוּהַ and שָׁחָה (compare שָׁכַךְ), pret. שַׁחֹותִי, plur. שַׁחוּ and שָׁחֲחוּ, fut. יָשׂחַ.

(1) to sink down, Hab 3:6 used of beasts of prey couching down and lying in wait in their lairs, Job 38:40.

(2) to be bowed down, cast down, Psa 10:10 107:39 Isa 2:11, 17 specially with sickness, Psa 35:14 38:7 Lam 3:20 also, to submit oneself. Isa 60:14, וְהָֽלְכוּ אֵלַיִךְ שְׁחֹוחַ “they shall come to thee bowing down.” Job 9:13 Pro 14:19.

Niphal, to be bowed down, Isa 2:9 5:15 . Used of a depressed and attenuated voice, Ecc 12:4. Pregn. Isa 29:4, וּמֵעָפָר תִּשַּׁח אִמְרָתֵךְ “and thy depressed (slender) voice shall be heard from the dust.”

Hiphil, to bring down, to humble, Isa 25:12 26:5 .

Hithpoel, to be cast down (the soul), Psa 42:7, 12 Ps4 2:12 43:5 .

Derivative, שַׁח.
